verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a soluble cytoplasmic chaperone at baseline but undergoes state-dependent surface translocation under heat shock, oxidative stress, and oncogenic transformation. Tumor cells constitutively display HSPA1A on the outer leaflet, where it is recognized by anti-Hsp70 antibodies and CAR-T programs. The cancer-cell ecto-pool is a documented, immunogenic surface-accessible form; this qualifies as cell-state-induced surface display. No intrinsic membrane anchor; surface residence is driven by translocation, not architecture.
